prompts: You are working in a fresh Vite + TypeScript workspace: config files only, there is no src/ directory yet — you will build the whole site from scratch. Over the next messages I will give you the requirements one at a time; do NOT write any code until I say so. Acknowledge in one sentence.

Read noise.txt in full with read_file. Then note this requirement (no code yet): the site is a hash-routed multi-page app; src/router.ts must export routeFor(path) returning {title, body} for known routes or null for unknown ones. Acknowledge in one sentence.

Read noise.txt in full with read_file. Then note this requirement (no code yet): the route '/' must have the title 'Orbit Books Home' and its body must include the text 'Welcome to Orbit Books'. Acknowledge in one sentence.

Read noise.txt in full with read_file. Then note this requirement (no code yet): the route '/catalog' must have the title 'Book Catalog' and its body must include 'Browse our shelves'. Acknowledge in one sentence.

Read noise.txt in full with read_file. Then note this requirement (no code yet): the route '/contact' must have the title 'Contact Orbit' and its body must include 'orbit@example.com'. Acknowledge in one sentence.

Read noise.txt in full with read_file. Then note this requirement (no code yet): src/catalog.ts must export formatBook(raw) mapping a raw record {title, price_cents, in_stock} to a view {title, price, availability}; 'title' passes through unchanged. Acknowledge in one sentence.

Read noise.txt in full with read_file. Then note this requirement (no code yet): in formatBook, 'price_cents' maps to 'price' as a dollar string with two decimals, e.g. 1999 becomes '$19.99' and 500 becomes '$5.00'. Acknowledge in one sentence.

Read noise.txt in full with read_file. Then note this requirement (no code yet): in formatBook, 'in_stock' true maps to availability 'In stock' and false maps to 'Out of stock'. Acknowledge in one sentence.

Read noise.txt in full with read_file. Then note this requirement (no code yet): you must also write your own test file src/catalog.test.ts with vitest, covering every route (including an unknown route returning null) and formatBook for both availability values. Acknowledge in one sentence.

Now build the whole site to satisfy EVERY requirement from the previous messages: create src/main.ts (render the current hash route into #app; on '/catalog' also fetch /catalog.json and append each book via formatBook), src/router.ts, src/catalog.ts, and src/catalog.test.ts. Use write_file to save each file. Then verify your work by running 'npx vitest run' and 'npx tsc --noEmit' with execute_command, fix any failures, and finish with a one-line summary.
